title: Project Manager – Mechanical & Civil Engineering (Tunnelling / Underground)
description_raw:
Glenigan Recruitment are looking for a Contract Project Manager with both mechanical and civil/structural experience to lead the mechanical and civils interface works on an underground tunnelling project in London. This is a multidisciplinary PM role with roughly equal emphasis on mechanical systems and civil structures.

Mechanical scope (primary):
- Flow control and isolation gates (large-bore slide gate and penstock systems)
- Pumping station mechanical fit-out (submersible sewage pumps, dry well mechanical services, HVAC for underground chambers)
- Screen and degritting mechanical equipment
- Odour control systems (biofilters, chemical scrubbers)

Civil / structural scope (secondary):
- Reinforced concrete shaft linings and chamber structures
- Structural works design review and buildability comments
- Grouting and ground treatment interface with tunnel boring machine (TBM) operations

Ideal background:
- CEng MIMechE (preferred) or MICE
- 10+ years infrastructure PM — must include 3+ years on mechanical plant delivery in tunnelling, water, or utilities
- NEC4 ECC administration
- PRINCE2 or APM PMQ
- Confined space medical clearance and working at height certification

Rate: £700 per day (outside IR35)
Duration: 14 months
Start: 17 August 2026
Location: Various tunnel drive sites across Greater London (travel allowance provided)
